chunks.en: I usually take a short break in the early afternoon.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい昼過ぎに短い休憩を取ります。

chunks.en: To relax, I stand up and do some simple stretching.
chunks.ja: リラックスするために、立ち上がって簡単なストレッチをします。

chunks.en: I also like to get a cup of coffee or some water.
chunks.ja: コーヒーか水を取りに行くのも好きです。

chunks.en: Sometimes I go to the break room to talk with my team.
chunks.ja: ときどき休憩室に行って、チームのみんなと話します。

chunks.en: When the weather is nice, I go outside for some fresh air.
chunks.ja: 天気がいいときは、外に出て新鮮な空気を吸います。

chunks.en: After a short break, I feel calm and ready to code again.
chunks.ja: 短い休憩のあと、私は落ち着いてまたコードを書く準備ができます。

outline: Opening: when I take my break
Point: what I do to relax and get a drink
Point: where I go, break room or outside
Wrap-up: how I feel after the break

modelTalk.text: I take short breaks a few times each day. My first break is in the morning, around ten. I feel a little tired, so I stop for a while. I stand up and stretch my arms. That feels really good after sitting for so long. Sometimes I walk to get a drink, like water or coffee. I like to go to the break room and sit down. I talk with my friends there for a few minutes. When it's sunny, I go outside for fresh air. I don't look at my phone much. I just close my eyes and breathe slowly. After a short break, I feel calm and happy again. My head feels clear, and I'm ready to work. Short breaks are simple, but they help me a lot every day.

chunks.en: I usually take a short break in the middle of the afternoon.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい午後の真ん中あたりに短い休憩を取ります。

chunks.en: What I do to relax is stretch my arms and get a cup of coffee.
chunks.ja: リラックスするためにやるのは、腕を伸ばしてコーヒーを一杯もらうことです。

chunks.en: Where I go is the break room, or sometimes I step outside for fresh air.
chunks.ja: 行く場所は休憩室で、たまに外に出て新鮮な空気を吸うこともあります。

chunks.en: After a short break, I feel more focused and ready to code again.
chunks.ja: 短い休憩のあとは、もっと集中できて、また開発に取りかかる準備ができます。

chunks.en: So taking small breaks really helps me stay calm during a busy day.
chunks.ja: だから小さな休憩を取ることは、忙しい一日でも落ち着いていられるように本当に役立ちます。

outline: Opening: when I take a break
Point 2: where I go (break room, outside)

modelTalk.text: I usually take my first short break in the late morning. By then, I've been at my desk for a while, and my eyes feel tired. So I stand up and stretch my arms and back. It feels really good. Then I get a drink, usually water or a warm coffee. Sometimes I walk to the break room and chat with a coworker. If the weather's nice, I go outside for a few minutes. I like the fresh air and the sound of the street. I don't check my phone much. I just try to relax and breathe slowly. After a short break, I feel calm and awake again. My head is clearer, and I can focus better. It's only five or ten minutes, but it really helps. So I try to do this a few times every day.
